subject: Social Science - History
chapter: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation

key_points: Before British rule, India had a widespread indigenous education system with institutions such as pathshalas, gurukuls, and madrassas.
British officials debated whether Indian education should focus on traditional learning or Western knowledge.
Orientalists supported the promotion of Indian languages like Sanskrit and Persian along with classical texts.
Anglicists believed that English education and Western science should be promoted in India.
Macaulay’s Minute (1835) supported English education and criticized traditional Indian knowledge systems.
Wood’s Despatch (1854) proposed a structured system of education with universities, colleges, and schools.
English education led to the emergence of an educated middle class in India.
Modern education introduced Western ideas such as liberty, equality, and democracy.
examples: Indigenous education example: Pathshalas teaching reading, writing, and arithmetic in villages.
Orientalist policy example: Support for Sanskrit colleges and classical Indian learning.
Anglicist policy example: Promotion of English language and Western science.
University establishment example: Universities of Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras founded in 1857.
Social reform example: Raja Rammohan Roy supporting Western scientific education.
important_terms: Indigenous Education: Traditional education system existing in India before British rule.
Orientalists: Scholars who believed Indian languages and classical learning should be promoted.
Anglicists: Supporters of English language education and Western knowledge.
Macaulay’s Minute (1835): Policy document supporting English education in India.
Wood’s Despatch (1854): Document that laid the foundation for modern education in India.
Educated Middle Class: A new social group created through English education.
exam_tips: Understand the differences between Orientalists and Anglicists.
Remember the significance of Macaulay’s Minute in promoting English education.
Know the main recommendations of Wood’s Despatch.
Remember the first modern universities established in 1857.
Link Western education with the rise of nationalism and social reform in India.
